name: scenario_616cda_marine_policy_school_district_s_new_marrowbridge_ind_col
premise: the incident command structure is under pressure in Q3 2025: 11 critical issues have surfaced over 12 hours, and the response will set a policy precedent for Marine Policy. The immediate incident is an operational integrity incident. The trigger is that competing obligations are colliding under time pressure, and the current playbook is not specific enough to be defensible. The board has pre-approved up to $4.5M in emergency spend if controls are documented, reversible, and auditable. Stakeholders frame this as individual vs collective: what looks efficient in aggregate may be unacceptable when it concentrates loss on a smaller group. Dashboards conflict with frontline notes, and vendor telemetry is delayed enough that decisions must be made on partial evidence. A decision is required before a same-day board briefing. Boundaries: Public communications must distinguish verified facts from hypotheses. The team must preserve evidence sufficient for an external audit. The response must include an appeals path usable without legal counsel. Whatever choice is made will be cited in later incidents as the default playbook for this domain.

agents.name: Response Commissioner Elliot Ibrahim
agents.identity: I am Response Commissioner Elliot Ibrahim, coordinating the Marine Policy response at Marrowbridge Marine Policy Coordination Cell. My mandate is to act fast without blurring accountability, to keep an auditable trail, and to make sure the costs of errors don’t land on people least able to contest them.
agents.goal: I want an authorization plan that preserves evidence, documents exceptions, and explicitly defines who gets relief first and why.

agents.name: Civil Liberties Counsel Keisha Kim
agents.identity: I am Civil Liberties Counsel Keisha Kim, coordinating the Marine Policy response at Marrowbridge Marine Policy Coordination Cell. My mandate is to act fast without blurring accountability, to keep an auditable trail, and to make sure the costs of errors don’t land on people least able to contest them.
agents.goal: I want safeguards that prevent a crisis workaround from becoming a permanent policy without oversight.

choices: I will authorize a narrow, time-boxed pilot first, with a reversible kill-switch, independent log capture, and an appeals channel usable without counsel; I will not scale until predefined safety and equity checks pass.
I will authorize a broad rollout immediately to reduce near-term harm, with transparent metrics and a rapid correction loop, accepting that some harms may surface only after precedent is set.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The window for a controlled decision is closing. Response Commissioner Elliot Ibrahim must choose an authorization approach for this Marine Policy decision. What does Response Commissioner Elliot Ibrahim do?
